title: Hypertension
subtitle: Smart Flow Revision
description: Understand the topic as a guided story, then reveal the full map.

nodes.title: Big Picture
nodes.summary: Hypertension is common, often silent, and dangerous when uncontrolled.
bullets: Affects a large number of adults
Raises risk of heart disease, stroke, and kidney disease
nodes.memoryHint: Remember it as common + silent + harmful.

nodes.title: Why Pharmacists Matter
nodes.summary: Pharmacists improve screening, monitoring, counseling, and adherence.
nodes.memoryHint: Pharmacists improve follow-up and treatment success.

nodes.title: Main Types
nodes.summary: Most cases are primary; secondary hypertension has an identifiable cause.
bullets: Secondary: due to renal disease, adrenal disease, OSA, or drugs
nodes.memoryHint: Primary = majority. Secondary = search for a cause.

nodes.title: Risk Factors for Primary Hypertension
nodes.summary: Primary hypertension usually develops in the presence of multiple risk factors.

nodes.title: Common Causes of Secondary Hypertension
nodes.summary: Secondary hypertension has a clear underlying driver.
nodes.memoryHint: If the cause is identifiable, think secondary HTN.

nodes.title: Core Pathophysiology
nodes.summary: Hypertension is driven mainly by SNS and RAAS activation.

nodes.title: RAAS Branch
nodes.summary: RAAS raises blood pressure through sodium retention and vasoconstriction.
bullets: Aldosterone increases sodium and water reabsorption
nodes.memoryHint: RAAS mainly increases volume and vascular tone.

nodes.title: SNS Branch
nodes.summary: SNS raises blood pressure through vessel constriction and heart stimulation.
bullets: Beta-1 stimulation increases heart rate and contractility
Higher SVR and cardiac output raise BP
nodes.memoryHint: SNS = tighter vessels + faster, stronger heart.

nodes.title: Hemodynamic Formula
nodes.summary: Blood pressure depends on vascular resistance and cardiac output.

nodes.title: Drugs That Can Increase Blood Pressure
nodes.summary: Some drugs and substances can worsen blood pressure control.
bullets: Sympathomimetics: ADHD drugs, decongestants, cocaine, caffeine
Other: oral contraceptives, VEGF inhibitors, ESAs
nodes.memoryHint: Always review the medication list in uncontrolled HTN.

nodes.title: Correct BP Monitoring
nodes.summary: Good diagnosis starts with good measurement technique.
bullets: Sit with back supported and feet on the floor
Support the arm at heart level
Avoid talking, smoking, caffeine, or exercise beforehand

nodes.title: Screening and Diagnosis
nodes.summary: Diagnosis requires repeated readings, not a single office value.
bullets: Use an average of at least 2 readings
Readings should be obtained on at least 2 occasions
White coat hypertension can mislead decisions

checkpoint.question: Is one office blood pressure reading enough to diagnose hypertension?
checkpoint.answer: No. Diagnosis should be based on repeated readings across separate occasions.

nodes.title: ACC/AHA BP Categories
nodes.summary: Blood pressure is classified into four major categories.

nodes.title: Lifestyle Management
nodes.summary: Lifestyle changes are essential in both prevention and treatment.
nodes.memoryHint: Lifestyle is always part of hypertension management.

nodes.title: Natural Products Pearl
nodes.summary: Natural products are not guideline-recommended for BP lowering.
nodes.memoryHint: Natural does not always mean harmless.
